---
title: "RI energy burden investigation (standalone)"
subtitle: "Low-income, gas-heated households under default vs. HP flat rates"
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
---

This notebook is a **standalone** copy of the energy burden analysis that lives as a fragment in [`investigate_RI_energy_burden.qmd`](investigate_RI_energy_burden.qmd) and as a section inside [`analysis.qmd`](analysis.qmd). Parameters, S3 paths, CPI inflation, master bill lazy frames, and plotting imports are inlined here so you can render or `quarto preview` this file without running the full RI analysis notebook.

**Scope:** LMI, gas-heated cohort; burden threshold 6%; scenarios = current HVAC (runs 1+2), HP + default (3+4), HP + HP flat rate (19+20).

## Setup

```{python}
#| label: setup

from __future__ import annotations

from pathlib import Path

import polars as pl
from lib.plotnine import SB_COLORS, theme_switchbox
from lib.quarto import display_svg
from plotnine import (
    aes,
    annotate,
    element_blank,
    geom_col,
    geom_hline,
    geom_text,
    ggplot,
    guides,
    labs,
    scale_fill_manual,
    scale_x_discrete,
    scale_y_continuous,
    theme,
)

report_vars: dict = {}
```

## Parameters

```{python}
#| label: params

# Keep in sync with `analysis.qmd` when re-pointing batches or years.
BATCH = "ri_20260330_170M-rr-and-all-topups_lmi_fix"
STATE = "RI"
YEAR = 2025

_state_lower = STATE.lower()
S3_BASE = "s3://data.sb/switchbox/cairo/outputs/hp_rates"
PATH_MASTER_BILLS_12 = f"{S3_BASE}/{_state_lower}/all_utilities/{BATCH}/run_1+2/comb_bills_year_target/"
PATH_MASTER_BILLS_34 = f"{S3_BASE}/{_state_lower}/all_utilities/{BATCH}/run_3+4/comb_bills_year_target/"
PATH_MASTER_BILLS_1920 = f"{S3_BASE}/{_state_lower}/all_utilities/{BATCH}/run_19+20/comb_bills_year_target/"


BATCH_NY = "ny_20260326_flex_r1-16"
PATH_MASTER_BILLS_12_NY = f"{S3_BASE}/ny/all_utilities/{BATCH_NY}/run_1+2/comb_bills_year_target/"
PATH_MASTER_BILLS_34_NY = f"{S3_BASE}/ny/all_utilities/{BATCH_NY}/run_3+4/comb_bills_year_target/"
PATH_MASTER_BILLS_1920_NY = f"{S3_BASE}/ny/all_utilities/{BATCH_NY}/run_19+20/comb_bills_year_target/"
PATH_ANNUAL_LOAD_CURVE_RI = "s3://data.sb/nrel/resstock/res_2024_amy2018_2/load_curve_annual/state=RI/upgrade=00/RI_upgrade00_metadata_and_annual_results.parquet"
PATH_ANNUAL_LOAD_CURVE_NY = "s3://data.sb/nrel/resstock/res_2024_amy2018_2/load_curve_annual/state=NY/upgrade=00/NY_upgrade00_metadata_and_annual_results.parquet"
PATH_CPI = "s3://data.sb/fred/cpi/"

```

## Constants

```{python}
#| label: constants

ANNUAL_MONTH = "Annual"
BLDG_ID = "bldg_id"
INCOME_COL = "in.representative_income"
RESSTOCK_INCOME_DOLLAR_YEAR = 2019
BURDEN_THRESHOLD = 0.06
LMI_CURRENT_ENROLLMENT_PCT = 32
report_vars["lmi_current_enrollment_pct"] = LMI_CURRENT_ENROLLMENT_PCT
LMI_BILL_COLS = ["elec_total_bill", "gas_total_bill", "oil_total_bill", "propane_total_bill"]
# RI master bills use LMI 32% and 100% tiers (no separate 40% column set).
LMI_40_BILL_COLS = ["elec_total_bill_lmi_32", "gas_total_bill_lmi_32", "oil_total_bill", "propane_total_bill"]
LMI_100_BILL_COLS = ["elec_total_bill_lmi_100", "gas_total_bill_lmi_100", "oil_total_bill", "propane_total_bill"]
UTIL_NAMES = {
    "rie": "Rhode Island Energy",
}
UTIL_ORDER_CODES = ["rie"]

LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_NET_ELECTRICITY_CONSUMPTION_COL = "out.electricity.net.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_FLOOR_AREA_COL = "out.params.floor_area_foundation_ft_2"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_COOLING_CAPACITY_COL = "out.params.size_cooling_system_primary_k_btu_h"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_COOLING_PEAK_COL = "out.load.cooling.peak.kbtu_hr"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_COOLING_DELIVERED_COL = "out.load.cooling.energy_delivered.kbtu"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_COOLING_CONSUMED_COL = "out.electricity.cooling.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_COOLING_FAN_CONSUMED_COL = "out.electricity.cooling_fans_pumps.energy_consumption.kwh"
# Derived in-notebook alias for cooling kWh + cooling fans/pumps kWh (not a parquet column).
COOLING_KWH_SUM_COL = "_cooling_plus_fan_kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_HEATING_CONSUMED_COL = "out.electricity.heating.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_HEATING_FAN_CONSUMED_COL = "out.electricity.heating_fans_pumps.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_BKUP_HEATING_CONSUMED_COL = "out.electricity.heating_hp_bkup.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_BKUP_HEATING_FAN_CONSUMED_COL = "out.electricity.heating_hp_bkup_fa.energy_consumption.kwh"
# Derived: heating + heating fans/pumps + HP backup + HP backup fans/pumps kWh (not a parquet column).
HEATING_KWH_SUM_COL = "_heating_fourway_kwh_sum"
HEATING_KWH_SUM_SOURCE_COLS = (
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_HEATING_CONSUMED_COL,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_HEATING_FAN_CONSUMED_COL,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_BKUP_HEATING_CONSUMED_COL,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_BKUP_HEATING_FAN_CONSUMED_COL,
)

LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_CEILING_FAN_COL = "out.electricity.ceiling_fan.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_CLOTHES_DRYER_COL = "out.electricity.clothes_dryer.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_CLOTHES_WASHER_COL = "out.electricity.clothes_washer.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_DISHWASHER_COL = "out.electricity.dishwasher.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_FREEZER_COL = "out.electricity.freezer.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_HOT_WATER_COL = "out.electricity.hot_water.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_EXTERIOR_LIGHT_COL = "out.electricity.lighting_exterior.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_GARAGE_LIGHT_COL = "out.electricity.lighting_garage.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_INTERIOR_LIGHT_COL = "out.electricity.lighting_interior.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_VENT_COL = "out.electricity.mech_vent.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_SPA_COL = "out.electricity.permanent_spa_heat.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_SPA_PUMP_COL = "out.electricity.permanent_spa_pump.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_PLUG_LOAD_COL = "out.electricity.plug_loads.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_PV_COL = "out.electricity.pv.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_OVEN_COL = "out.electricity.range_oven.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_FRIDGE_COL = "out.electricity.refrigerator.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_WELL_PUMP_COL = "out.electricity.well_pump.energy_consumption.kwh"

# Appliance annual kWh columns split into three groups (partition of the former bundle).
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_APPLIANCES_PV_COLS = (LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_PV_COL,)
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_APPLIANCES_LAUNDRY_DHW_PLUG_COLS = (
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_CLOTHES_DRYER_COL,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_CLOTHES_WASHER_COL,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_DISHWASHER_COL,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_HOT_WATER_COL,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_PLUG_LOAD_COL,
)
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_APPLIANCES_OTHER_COLS = (
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_CEILING_FAN_COL,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_FREEZER_COL,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_EXTERIOR_LIGHT_COL,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_GARAGE_LIGHT_COL,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_INTERIOR_LIGHT_COL,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_VENT_COL,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_SPA_COL,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_SPA_PUMP_COL,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_OVEN_COL,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_FRIDGE_COL,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_WELL_PUMP_COL,
)
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_APPLIANCES_COLS = (
    *LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_APPLIANCES_PV_COLS,
    *LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_APPLIANCES_LAUNDRY_DHW_PLUG_COLS,
    *LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_APPLIANCES_OTHER_COLS,
)
# Derived category sums and total (not parquet columns).
APPLIANCES_PV_KWH_SUM_COL = "_appliances_pv_kwh_sum"
APPLIANCES_LAUNDRY_DHW_PLUG_KWH_SUM_COL = "_appliances_laundry_dhw_plug_kwh_sum"
APPLIANCES_OTHER_KWH_SUM_COL = "_appliances_other_kwh_sum"
APPLIANCES_KWH_SUM_COL = "_appliances_bundle_kwh_sum"


# Natural gas total
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_NAT_GAS_TOTAL = "out.natural_gas.total.energy_consumption.kwh"

# Natural gas appliances
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_NAT_GAS_DRYER = "out.natural_gas.clothes_dryer.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_NAT_GAS_FIREPLACE = "out.natural_gas.fireplace.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_NAT_GAS_GRILL = "out.natural_gas.grill.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_NAT_GAS_HOT_WATER = "out.natural_gas.hot_water.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_NAT_GAS_LIGHTING = "out.natural_gas.lighting.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_NAT_GAS_SPA_HEAT = "out.natural_gas.permanent_spa_heat.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_NAT_GAS_POOL_HEATER = "out.natural_gas.pool_heater.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_NAT_GAS_RANGE_OVEN = "out.natural_gas.range_oven.energy_consumption.kwh"


# Natural gas HVAC
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_NAT_GAS_HVAC_HEATING = "out.natural_gas.heating.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_NAT_GAS_HVAC_HEATING_BKUP = "out.natural_gas.heating_hp_bkup.energy_consumption.kwh"

LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_NAT_GAS_APPLIANCES_COLS = (
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_NAT_GAS_DRYER,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_NAT_GAS_FIREPLACE,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_NAT_GAS_GRILL,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_NAT_GAS_HOT_WATER,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_NAT_GAS_LIGHTING,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_NAT_GAS_SPA_HEAT,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_NAT_GAS_POOL_HEATER,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_NAT_GAS_RANGE_OVEN,
)
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_NAT_GAS_HVAC_COLS = (
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_NAT_GAS_HVAC_HEATING,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_NAT_GAS_HVAC_HEATING_BKUP,
)

# Propane total
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_PROPANE_TOTAL = "out.propane.total.energy_consumption.kwh"

# Propane appliances
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_PROPANE_DRYER = "out.propane.clothes_dryer.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_PROPANE_HOT_WATER = "out.propane.hot_water.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_PROPANE_RANGE_OVEN = "out.propane.range_oven.energy_consumption.kwh"

# Propane HVAC
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_PROPANE_HVAC_HEATING = "out.propane.heating.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_PROPANE_HVAC_HEATING_BKUP = "out.propane.heating_hp_bkup.energy_consumption.kwh"

LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_PROPANE_APPLIANCES_COLS = (
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_PROPANE_DRYER,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_PROPANE_HOT_WATER,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_PROPANE_RANGE_OVEN,
)
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_PROPANE_HVAC_COLS = (
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_PROPANE_HVAC_HEATING,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_PROPANE_HVAC_HEATING_BKUP,
)

# Fuel oil total
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_FUEL_OIL_TOTAL = "out.fuel_oil.total.energy_consumption.kwh"

# Fuel oil appliances
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_FUEL_OIL_HOT_WATER = "out.fuel_oil.hot_water.energy_consumption.kwh"

# Fuel oil HVAC
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_FUEL_OIL_HVAC_HEATING = "out.fuel_oil.heating.energy_consumption.kwh"
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_FUEL_OIL_HVAC_HEATING_BKUP = "out.fuel_oil.heating_hp_bkup.energy_consumption.kwh"

LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_FUEL_OIL_APPLIANCES_COLS = (LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_FUEL_OIL_HOT_WATER,)
LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_FUEL_OIL_HVAC_COLS = (
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_FUEL_OIL_HVAC_HEATING,
    LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_FUEL_OIL_HVAC_HEATING_BKUP,
)


```

## Data ingest

```{python}
#| label: ingest

bills_current = pl.scan_parquet(PATH_MASTER_BILLS_12, hive_partitioning=True)
bills_hp = pl.scan_parquet(PATH_MASTER_BILLS_34, hive_partitioning=True)
bills_hprate_hp = pl.scan_parquet(PATH_MASTER_BILLS_1920, hive_partitioning=True)
load_curve_annual_ri = pl.scan_parquet(PATH_ANNUAL_LOAD_CURVE_RI, hive_partitioning=True)
load_curve_annual_ny = pl.scan_parquet(PATH_ANNUAL_LOAD_CURVE_NY, hive_partitioning=True)

bills_current_ny = pl.scan_parquet(PATH_MASTER_BILLS_12_NY, hive_partitioning=True)
bills_hp_ny = pl.scan_parquet(PATH_MASTER_BILLS_34_NY, hive_partitioning=True)
bills_hprate_hp_ny = pl.scan_parquet(PATH_MASTER_BILLS_1920_NY, hive_partitioning=True)
```

```{python}
#| label: sample-size

_sample = (
    bills_current.filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH).select(BLDG_ID, "weight").collect()
)
n_resstock_bldgs = _sample[BLDG_ID].n_unique()
n_rie_customers = int(round(_sample["weight"].sum()))

print(f"RI annual master bills — unique buildings: {n_resstock_bldgs:,} (all buildings; not the analysis cohort).")
print(f"RI sum of weights (all annual rows): {n_rie_customers:,}")
print("All plots and tables below use the LMI + gas-heated cohort only (defined after CPI ingest).")
```

```{python}
#| label: ingest-cpi

_cpi = pl.read_parquet(PATH_CPI)
_cpi_from = float(_cpi.filter(pl.col("year") == RESSTOCK_INCOME_DOLLAR_YEAR)["value"][0])
_cpi_to = float(_cpi.filter(pl.col("year") == YEAR)["value"][0])
INCOME_CPI_RATIO = _cpi_to / _cpi_from
print(f"CPI inflation ratio {RESSTOCK_INCOME_DOLLAR_YEAR}→{YEAR}: {INCOME_CPI_RATIO:.4f}")
```

## Energy burden for low-income gas-heated households

What share of low-income, gas-heated households face a high energy burden — defined as spending more than 6% of annual
income on energy — and how does that share change when they switch to heat pumps under different rate designs?

We focus on low-and-moderate-income (LMI) households — identified by the `is_lmi` flag in the master bill tables —
who heat with natural gas in the baseline scenario (upgrade 0). We track the same cohort of buildings across three
scenarios:

1. **Current HVAC, default rates** (runs 1+2) — the status quo
2. **Heat pump, default rates** (runs 3+4) — electrified but on flat rates
3. **Heat pump, HP flat rates** (runs 19+20) — electrified on the proposed HP flat rate

```{python}
#| label: burden-cohort

low_income_gas_bldgs = (
    bills_current.filter(
        (pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
        & pl.col("heats_with_natgas")
        & pl.col("is_lmi_any")
        & (pl.col(INCOME_COL) > 0)
    )
    .select(BLDG_ID, "weight", "energy_total_bill", INCOME_COL, "sb.electric_utility")
    .collect()
)

low_income_gas_bldgs_ny = (
    bills_current_ny.filter(
        (pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
        & pl.col("heats_with_natgas")
        & pl.col("is_lmi_any")
        & (pl.col(INCOME_COL) > 0)
    )
    .select(BLDG_ID, "weight", "energy_total_bill", INCOME_COL, "sb.electric_utility")
    .collect()
)

cohort_bldg_ids = low_income_gas_bldgs[BLDG_ID].to_list()
cohort_bldg_ids_ny = low_income_gas_bldgs_ny[BLDG_ID].to_list()
n_cohort = len(cohort_bldg_ids)
n_cohort_weighted = low_income_gas_bldgs["weight"].sum()
n_cohort_ny = len(cohort_bldg_ids_ny)
n_cohort_weighted_ny = low_income_gas_bldgs_ny["weight"].sum()

print(
    f"LMI + gas-heated cohort — RI: {n_cohort:,} buildings (weighted customers ≈ {n_cohort_weighted:,.0f})  |  "
    f"NY: {n_cohort_ny:,} (≈ {n_cohort_weighted_ny:,.0f})",
)
```

```{python}
#| label: burden-compute


def burden_shares(
    bills_lf: pl.LazyFrame,
    bldg_ids: list,
    income_df: pl.DataFrame,
    bill_cols: list[str] = LMI_BILL_COLS,
    burden_threshold: float = BURDEN_THRESHOLD,
) -> tuple[float, float]:
    """Return (weighted_pct_below, weighted_pct_above) the burden threshold.

    bill_cols controls which columns are summed to form the total energy bill.
    Default uses the undiscounted columns; pass LMI_40_BILL_COLS or
    LMI_100_BILL_COLS to use LMI-discounted bills.

    Income is inflated from ResStock's 2019$ to the current year via
    INCOME_CPI_RATIO so that burden = bill / income uses consistent dollars.
    """
    annual = (
        bills_lf.filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
        .select(BLDG_ID, "weight", *bill_cols)
        .collect()
        .filter(pl.col(BLDG_ID).is_in(bldg_ids))
        .with_columns(pl.sum_horizontal(bill_cols).alias("total_bill"))
        .join(income_df.select(BLDG_ID, INCOME_COL), on=BLDG_ID, how="inner")
        .with_columns(
            (pl.col("total_bill") / (pl.col(INCOME_COL) * INCOME_CPI_RATIO)).alias("burden"),
        )
        .with_columns((pl.col("burden") > burden_threshold).alias("is_burdened"))
    )
    total_weight = annual["weight"].sum()
    burdened_weight = annual.filter(pl.col("is_burdened"))["weight"].sum()
    pct_above = burdened_weight / total_weight * 100
    pct_below = 100 - pct_above
    return (pct_below, pct_above)


income_df = low_income_gas_bldgs.select(BLDG_ID, INCOME_COL)

below_current, above_current = burden_shares(bills_current, cohort_bldg_ids, income_df)
below_hp_default, above_hp_default = burden_shares(bills_hp, cohort_bldg_ids, income_df)
below_hp_flat, above_hp_flat = burden_shares(bills_hprate_hp, cohort_bldg_ids, income_df)

print(f"Current HVAC:        {above_current:.1f}% above 6% | {below_current:.1f}% below")
print(f"HP + default rates:  {above_hp_default:.1f}% above 6% | {below_hp_default:.1f}% below")
print(f"HP + flat rates: {above_hp_flat:.1f}% above 6% | {below_hp_flat:.1f}% below")
```

```{python}
#| label: burden-bar-helper

BURDEN_SCENARIO_LABELS = [
    f"Current HVAC\nDefault Rates\n~{LMI_CURRENT_ENROLLMENT_PCT}% LMI Discounts",
    f"Heat Pump\nDefault Rates\n~{LMI_CURRENT_ENROLLMENT_PCT}% LMI Discounts",
    f"Heat Pump\nHP Flat Rates\n~{LMI_CURRENT_ENROLLMENT_PCT}% LMI Discounts",
    "Heat Pump\nHP Flat Rates\n100% LMI Discounts",
]
BURDEN_SEGMENT_ORDER = ["Above 6%", "Below 6%"]
BURDEN_COLORS = {
    "Above 6%": SB_COLORS["carrot"],
    "Below 6%": SB_COLORS["sky"],
}


def plot_burden_bar(
    below_pcts: list[float],
    above_pcts: list[float],
    scenario_labels: list[str] = BURDEN_SCENARIO_LABELS,
):
    """Diverging bar chart of burden shares centered on the 6% threshold.

    Orange bars (above 6%) extend upward from y=0; blue bars (below 6%)
    extend downward. The y=0 line represents the burden threshold.
    """
    rows = []
    for scenario, above, below in zip(scenario_labels, above_pcts, below_pcts):
        rows.append({
            "scenario": scenario,
            "segment": "Above 6%",
            "value": above,
            "label": f"{round(above)}%",
            "label_y": above / 2,
        })
        rows.append({
            "scenario": scenario,
            "segment": "Below 6%",
            "value": -below,
            "label": f"{round(below)}%",
            "label_y": -below / 2,
        })

    df = pl.DataFrame(rows).with_columns(
        pl.col("scenario").cast(pl.Enum(scenario_labels)),
        pl.col("segment").cast(pl.Enum(BURDEN_SEGMENT_ORDER)),
    )

    return (
        ggplot(df, aes(x="scenario", y="value", fill="segment"))
        + geom_col(width=0.5)
        + geom_text(
            aes(y="label_y", label="label"),
            color="white",
            fontweight="bold",
            size=11,
        )
        + geom_hline(yintercept=0, size=1.5, color="#333333")
        + annotate(
            "text",
            x=0.45,
            y=0,
            label="6% Burden\nThreshold",
            ha="right",
            va="center",
            fontweight="bold",
            size=11,
            color="#333333",
            family="IBM Plex Sans",
        )
        + scale_fill_manual(values=BURDEN_COLORS)
        + scale_x_discrete(limits=scenario_labels, expand=(0, 0.8, 0, 0.5))
        + scale_y_continuous(
            expand=(0.05, 0, 0.05, 0),
            labels=lambda xs: ["" for _ in xs],
        )
        + guides(fill=False)
        + labs(x="", y="")
        + theme_switchbox()
        + theme(
            figure_size=(10, 7),
            axis_text_y=element_blank(),
            axis_ticks_major_y=element_blank(),
            axis_ticks_minor_y=element_blank(),
            axis_line_y=element_blank(),
            panel_grid=element_blank(),
            axis_line_x=element_blank(),
            axis_ticks_major_x=element_blank(),
        )
    )
```

### With current LMI discount enrollment (tier in `LMI_40_BILL_COLS`)

For Rhode Island, master bills expose a **32%** enrolled LMI tier (`elec_total_bill_lmi_32` / `gas_total_bill_lmi_32`). Scenario labels use `LMI_CURRENT_ENROLLMENT_PCT` (32) for display consistency with `analysis.qmd`.

```{python}
#| label: burden-compute-lmi-current

below_current_lmi40, above_current_lmi40 = burden_shares(
    bills_current, cohort_bldg_ids, income_df, bill_cols=LMI_40_BILL_COLS,
)
below_hp_default_lmi40, above_hp_default_lmi40 = burden_shares(
    bills_hp, cohort_bldg_ids, income_df, bill_cols=LMI_40_BILL_COLS,
)
below_hp_flat_lmi40, above_hp_flat_lmi40 = burden_shares(
    bills_hprate_hp, cohort_bldg_ids, income_df, bill_cols=LMI_40_BILL_COLS,
)
below_hp_flat_lmi100, above_hp_flat_lmi100 = burden_shares(
    bills_hprate_hp, cohort_bldg_ids, income_df, bill_cols=LMI_100_BILL_COLS,
)

print(f"Current HVAC (32% enrolled):         {above_current_lmi40:.1f}% above 6%")
print(f"HP + default rates (32% enrolled):   {above_hp_default_lmi40:.1f}% above 6%")
print(f"HP + flat rates (32% enrolled):  {above_hp_flat_lmi40:.1f}% above 6%")
print(f"HP + flat rates (100% enrolled): {above_hp_flat_lmi100:.1f}% above 6%")

report_vars["pct_burdened_hp_flat_lmi100"] = above_hp_flat_lmi100 / 100
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-burden-bar-lmi-current
#| fig-cap: "Share of low-income gas-heated households above the 6% energy burden threshold"

plot_burden_bar(
    [below_current_lmi40, below_hp_default_lmi40, below_hp_flat_lmi40, below_hp_flat_lmi100],
    [above_current_lmi40, above_hp_default_lmi40, above_hp_flat_lmi40, above_hp_flat_lmi100],
)
```

#### By utility

```{python}
#| label: burden-compute-lmi-current-by-utility

burden_by_utility: dict[str, dict[str, tuple[float, float]]] = {}
for code in UTIL_ORDER_CODES:
    util_bldg_ids = low_income_gas_bldgs.filter(pl.col("sb.electric_utility") == code)[BLDG_ID].to_list()
    if len(util_bldg_ids) < 10:
        continue
    util_income = income_df.filter(pl.col(BLDG_ID).is_in(util_bldg_ids))
    burden_by_utility[code] = {
        "current": burden_shares(bills_current, util_bldg_ids, util_income, bill_cols=LMI_40_BILL_COLS),
        "hp_default": burden_shares(bills_hp, util_bldg_ids, util_income, bill_cols=LMI_40_BILL_COLS),
        "hp_flat": burden_shares(bills_hprate_hp, util_bldg_ids, util_income, bill_cols=LMI_40_BILL_COLS),
        "hp_flat_lmi100": burden_shares(bills_hprate_hp, util_bldg_ids, util_income, bill_cols=LMI_100_BILL_COLS),
    }

for code, data in burden_by_utility.items():
    print(
        f"{UTIL_NAMES[code]:20s}  Current: {data['current'][1]:5.1f}%  "
        f"HP default: {data['hp_default'][1]:5.1f}%  HP flat: {data['hp_flat'][1]:5.1f}%  "
        f"HP flat+100%LMI: {data['hp_flat_lmi100'][1]:5.1f}%",
    )
```

```{python}
#| label: burden-bar-by-utility-helper

_BBU_BAR_HEIGHT = 0.55
_BBU_GROUP_GAP = 0.7
_BBU_INTRA_GAP = 0.15
_BBU_HEADER_SPACE = 0.55
_BBU_LABEL_SPACE = 0.35

_BURDEN_LABEL_ABOVE = "ENERGY BURDEN ABOVE 6%"
_BURDEN_LABEL_BELOW = "ENERGY BURDEN BELOW 6%"

_BURDEN_UTIL_SCENARIO_KEYS = ["current", "hp_default", "hp_flat", "hp_flat_lmi100"]
_BURDEN_UTIL_SCENARIO_LABELS = [
    f"Current HVAC\nDefault Rates\n~{LMI_CURRENT_ENROLLMENT_PCT}% LMI Discounts",
    f"Heat Pump\nDefault Rates\n~{LMI_CURRENT_ENROLLMENT_PCT}% LMI Discounts",
    f"Heat Pump\nHP Flat Rates\n~{LMI_CURRENT_ENROLLMENT_PCT}% LMI Discounts",
    "Heat Pump\nHP Flat Rates\n100% LMI Discounts",
]


def plot_burden_bar_by_utility(
    burden_data: dict[str, dict[str, tuple[float, float]]],
    utility_names: dict[str, str],
    scenario_keys: list[str] = _BURDEN_UTIL_SCENARIO_KEYS,
    scenario_labels: list[str] = _BURDEN_UTIL_SCENARIO_LABELS,
    title: str = "",
) -> None:
    """Diverging burden bars (above/below 6%) for each utility.

    Displays as inline SVG via IPython so Quarto's ``embed`` shortcode works.
    """
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

    ordered_codes = list(reversed(UTIL_ORDER_CODES))
    active_codes = [c for c in ordered_codes if c in burden_data]
    n_utils = len(active_codes)
    n_scenarios = len(scenario_keys)

    bar_positions: list[float] = []
    bar_labels: list[str] = []
    bar_above: list[float] = []
    bar_below: list[float] = []
    utility_header_ys: list[tuple[float, str]] = []
    label_rows: list[tuple[float, float, float]] = []

    y = 0.0
    for u_idx, code in enumerate(active_codes):
        utility_header_ys.append((y, utility_names[code]))
        y += _BBU_HEADER_SPACE

        first_below, first_above = burden_data[code][scenario_keys[0]]
        label_rows.append((y, first_above, first_below))
        y += _BBU_LABEL_SPACE

        for s_idx, key in enumerate(scenario_keys):
            below, above = burden_data[code][key]
            bar_positions.append(y)
            bar_labels.append(scenario_labels[s_idx])
            bar_above.append(above)
            bar_below.append(below)
            y += _BBU_BAR_HEIGHT + (_BBU_INTRA_GAP if s_idx < n_scenarios - 1 else 0)
        if u_idx < n_utils - 1:
            y += _BBU_GROUP_GAP

    y_max = y + 0.1
    fig_height = 1.5 + n_utils * 3.6

    fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(12.5, fig_height))
    ax.set_xlim(-100, 100)
    ax.set_ylim(-0.1, y_max)
    ax.invert_yaxis()
    ax.set_xticks([])
    ax.set_yticks([])
    for spine in ax.spines.values():
        spine.set_visible(False)

    carrot = BURDEN_COLORS["Above 6%"]
    sky = BURDEN_COLORS["Below 6%"]

    for bp, above, below in zip(bar_positions, bar_above, bar_below):
        ax.barh(bp, above, left=0, height=_BBU_BAR_HEIGHT, color=carrot, edgecolor="none", align="edge")
        ax.barh(bp, -below, left=0, height=_BBU_BAR_HEIGHT, color=sky, edgecolor="none", align="edge")
        if above >= 3:
            ax.text(
                above / 2,
                bp + _BBU_BAR_HEIGHT / 2,
                f"{round(above)}%",
                ha="center",
                va="center",
                color="white",
                fontsize=10,
                fontweight="bold",
                fontfamily="IBM Plex Sans",
            )
        if below >= 3:
            ax.text(
                -below / 2,
                bp + _BBU_BAR_HEIGHT / 2,
                f"{round(below)}%",
                ha="center",
                va="center",
                color="white",
                fontsize=10,
                fontweight="bold",
                fontfamily="IBM Plex Sans",
            )

    for bp, label in zip(bar_positions, bar_labels):
        ax.text(
            101,
            bp + _BBU_BAR_HEIGHT / 2,
            label,
            ha="left",
            va="center",
            fontsize=8,
            fontweight="bold",
            fontfamily="IBM Plex Sans",
        )

    for header_y, util_name in utility_header_ys:
        ax.text(
            -100,
            header_y,
            util_name,
            ha="left",
            va="top",
            fontsize=11,
            fontweight="bold",
            fontfamily="GT Planar",
            color="#333333",
        )

    ax.axvline(x=0, color="#333333", linewidth=1.5)

    for label_y, first_above, first_below in label_rows:
        ax.text(
            first_above / 2,
            label_y,
            _BURDEN_LABEL_ABOVE,
            ha="center",
            va="bottom",
            fontsize=8,
            fontweight="bold",
            color=carrot,
            fontfamily="IBM Plex Sans",
        )
        ax.text(
            -first_below / 2,
            label_y,
            _BURDEN_LABEL_BELOW,
            ha="center",
            va="bottom",
            fontsize=8,
            fontweight="bold",
            color=sky,
            fontfamily="IBM Plex Sans",
        )

    fig.subplots_adjust(left=0.02, right=0.82, top=0.95, bottom=0.01)

    if title:
        ax_pos = ax.get_position()
        fig.text(
            ax_pos.x0,
            0.97,
            title,
            fontsize=12,
            fontweight="bold",
            fontfamily="GT Planar",
            color="black",
            va="top",
        )

    display_svg(fig)
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-burden-bar-lmi-current-by-utility
#| fig-cap: "Share of low-income gas-heated households above the 6% energy burden threshold by utility, with current LMI discount enrollment (~32% RI tier)"

plot_burden_bar_by_utility(
    burden_by_utility,
    UTIL_NAMES,
    title="Energy burdens for LMI customers, by utility",
)
```

```{python}
#| label: lmi-bill-share-burden-build

_LMI_BILL_PCT_NAMES = ("pct_elec", "pct_gas", "pct_oil", "pct_propane")
_LMI_BILL_LABELS = ("Electricity", "Natural gas", "Fuel oil", "Propane")


def _lmi_bill_share_with_burden(path: str, bldg_ids: list) -> pl.DataFrame:
    """Annual rows for ``bldg_ids`` with positive four-fuel bill sum and positive income; burden vs threshold."""
    tb = pl.sum_horizontal([pl.col(c).fill_null(0) for c in LMI_BILL_COLS])
    return (
        pl.scan_parquet(path, hive_partitioning=True)
        .filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
        .filter(pl.col(BLDG_ID).is_in(bldg_ids))
        .select(BLDG_ID, INCOME_COL, *LMI_BILL_COLS)
        .with_columns(tb.alias("_bill_sum_four"))
        .filter(pl.col("_bill_sum_four") > 0)
        .filter(pl.col(INCOME_COL).fill_null(0) > 0)
        .with_columns(
            (pl.col("_bill_sum_four") / (pl.col(INCOME_COL) * INCOME_CPI_RATIO)).alias("_burden"),
        )
        .with_columns((pl.col("_burden") > BURDEN_THRESHOLD).alias("is_energy_burdened"))
        .with_columns(
            [
                (100 * pl.col(LMI_BILL_COLS[i]).fill_null(0) / pl.col("_bill_sum_four")).alias(
                    _LMI_BILL_PCT_NAMES[i]
                )
                for i in range(len(LMI_BILL_COLS))
            ]
        )
        .collect()
    )


_lmi_bill_share_burden_ri = _lmi_bill_share_with_burden(PATH_MASTER_BILLS_12, cohort_bldg_ids)
_lmi_bill_share_burden_ny = _lmi_bill_share_with_burden(PATH_MASTER_BILLS_12_NY, cohort_bldg_ids_ny)

for _st, _df in (("RI", _lmi_bill_share_burden_ri), ("NY", _lmi_bill_share_burden_ny)):
    _n_b = _df.filter(pl.col("is_energy_burdened")).height
    _n_nb = _df.filter(~pl.col("is_energy_burdened")).height
    print(
        f"{_st}: income>0 & four-fuel sum>0: {_df.height:,}  |  "
        f"burdened (>{BURDEN_THRESHOLD:.0%} of CPI-adjusted income): {_n_b:,}  |  "
        f"not burdened: {_n_nb:,}",
    )
```

## Diagnosing the RI vs. NY energy burden gap

**Cohort:** **LMI + gas-heated** buildings — run **1+2** master bills, **`month == Annual`**, **positive sum of `LMI_BILL_COLS`**, **positive `in.representative_income`** — same frames as `_lmi_bill_share_burden_ri` / `_ny` (computed in the previous chunk). **Burden** is `_bill_sum_four ÷ (income × INCOME_CPI_RATIO)`; **energy burdened** is burden **>** `BURDEN_THRESHOLD` (**6%**). Percentages are **unweighted** shares of buildings (no ResStock weights in these tables). The **headline table** is next; **pooled income deciles** and the **mean bill bridge** appear **after** the LMI bill-mix figures below.

**How to read the headline row:** Energy burden is a **ratio of bills to income**. When **median and mean `in.representative_income` are higher for New York than for Rhode Island** in the table below, while **median and mean total bills are similar or not much lower in NY**, the main driver of Rhode Island’s **higher % above the 6% threshold** and **higher median burden %** is typically **income composition within the LMI cohort**: modeled LMI gas-heated homes in NY sit **higher in nominal income** (still LMI) than their RI counterparts, so the **same or a modestly lower bill** translates into a **lower burden percentage** in NY. Bills still matter, but the headline contrast is **largely a denominator story**—**lower representative income in RI**—not only higher energy charges. The pooled income-decile table later in this notebook holds income bands fixed across states to separate bill effects from that composition effect.

```{python}
#| label: tbl-burden-ri-ny-headline-summary
#| tbl-cap: "Headline comparison: burden prevalence, median burden, bills, and income — RI vs. NY (LMI + gas-heated cohort)."

from great_tables import GT, md

_burden_diag_combo = pl.concat(
    [
        _lmi_bill_share_burden_ri.with_columns(pl.lit("Rhode Island").alias("State")),
        _lmi_bill_share_burden_ny.with_columns(pl.lit("New York").alias("State")),
    ],
    how="vertical",
)

_tbl_headline = (
    _burden_diag_combo.group_by("State")
    .agg(
        pl.len().alias("N_buildings"),
        (100.0 * pl.col("is_energy_burdened").cast(pl.Float64).mean()).alias("pct_burdened"),
        (100.0 * pl.col("_burden").median()).alias("median_burden_pct"),
        pl.col("_bill_sum_four").median().alias("median_total_bill"),
        pl.col("_bill_sum_four").mean().alias("mean_total_bill"),
        pl.col(INCOME_COL).median().alias("median_income_resstock"),
        pl.col(INCOME_COL).mean().alias("mean_income_resstock"),
    )
    .sort("State", descending=True)
)

_gt_headline = (
    GT(_tbl_headline)
    .tab_header(
        title="RI vs. NY — headline burden diagnostics",
        subtitle=md(
            f"Income is `in.representative_income` (ResStock {RESSTOCK_INCOME_DOLLAR_YEAR}$). "
            f"Bills are undiscounted sum of `LMI_BILL_COLS` ($/yr). "
            f"Median/mean burden shown as % of CPI-adjusted income (×{INCOME_CPI_RATIO:.4f})."
        ),
    )
    .fmt_integer(columns="N_buildings")
    .fmt_number(columns=["pct_burdened", "median_burden_pct"], decimals=1)
    .fmt_currency(
        columns=["median_total_bill", "mean_total_bill", "median_income_resstock", "mean_income_resstock"],
        decimals=0,
    )
    .cols_label(
        N_buildings=md("*N*"),
        pct_burdened=md("% burdened<br>(>6%)"),
        median_burden_pct=md("Median<br>burden %"),
        median_total_bill=md("Median<br>total bill"),
        mean_total_bill=md("Mean<br>total bill"),
        median_income_resstock=md("Median<br>income"),
        mean_income_resstock=md("Mean<br>income"),
    )
)

_gt_headline
```

```{python}
#| label: print-headline-interpretation

_r = _tbl_headline.filter(pl.col("State") == "Rhode Island").to_dicts()[0]
_n = _tbl_headline.filter(pl.col("State") == "New York").to_dicts()[0]
print("\n--- Headline gap (RI minus NY) ---")
print(
    f"  % burdened:     {_r['pct_burdened'] - _n['pct_burdened']:+.1f} pp "
    f"  (RI {_r['pct_burdened']:.1f}%  vs  NY {_n['pct_burdened']:.1f}%)",
)
print(
    f"  Median burden:  {_r['median_burden_pct'] - _n['median_burden_pct']:+.2f} pp of income "
    f"  (RI {_r['median_burden_pct']:.2f}%  vs  NY {_n['median_burden_pct']:.2f}%)",
)
print(
    f"  Median bill:    ${_r['median_total_bill'] - _n['median_total_bill']:+,.0f}/yr "
    f"  (RI ${_r['median_total_bill']:,.0f}  vs  NY ${_n['median_total_bill']:,.0f})",
)
print(
    f"  Median income:  ${_r['median_income_resstock'] - _n['median_income_resstock']:+,.0f} "
    f"(ResStock $; same CPI scaling applies to both)",
)
_d_med_bill = float(_r["median_total_bill"] - _n["median_total_bill"])
_d_med_inc = float(_r["median_income_resstock"] - _n["median_income_resstock"])
if abs(_d_med_inc) < 500:
    print(
        "\n  Note: median incomes are similar; a large burden gap with small income gap "
        "usually points to the **bill side** (rates, use, fuel mix), not poorer households in-sample.",
    )
```

## Representative annual income (RI vs. NY)

Each panel is the distribution of **`in.representative_income`** for the **LMI + gas-heated cohort** (`low_income_gas_bldgs` / `low_income_gas_bldgs_ny`), run **1+2** annual master bills. Values are in **ResStock nominal dollars** (see `RESSTOCK_INCOME_DOLLAR_YEAR`). The **horizontal axis uses the same range in both panels**: upper limit from the **pooled 99.5th percentile** across RI and NY cohorts (rounded up to the nearest $5,000); incomes above that appear in the rightmost bin.

```{python}
#| label: fig-representative-income-ri-ny
#| fig-cap: "LMI + gas-heated cohort: distribution of `in.representative_income`, RI vs. NY. Shared x-axis (pooled 99.5th percentile, rounded); y-axis is percent of buildings in that state."

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

_income_dist_ri = low_income_gas_bldgs.select(BLDG_ID, INCOME_COL)
_income_dist_ny = low_income_gas_bldgs_ny.select(BLDG_ID, INCOME_COL)

_ri_inc = np.asarray(_income_dist_ri[INCOME_COL].to_numpy(), dtype=np.float64)
_ny_inc = np.asarray(_income_dist_ny[INCOME_COL].to_numpy(), dtype=np.float64)
_pooled = np.concatenate([_ri_inc, _ny_inc])
_hi_pct = float(np.percentile(_pooled, 99.5))
_xmax = float(max(np.ceil(_hi_pct / 5000.0) * 5000.0, 10_000.0))
_bin_width = max(2500.0, _xmax / 40.0)
_edges = np.arange(0, _xmax + _bin_width, _bin_width)

fig, axes = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(11, 4.2), sharex=True, sharey=False)
_body = "IBM Plex Sans"
_title = "GT Planar"
for ax, vals, state_name, color, n_b in zip(
    axes,
    (_ri_inc, _ny_inc),
    ("Rhode Island", "New York"),
    (SB_COLORS["sky"], SB_COLORS["carrot"]),
    (_ri_inc.size, _ny_inc.size),
):
    v = np.clip(vals, 0.0, _xmax)
    n = int(v.size)
    if n > 0:
        w = np.full(n, 100.0 / n, dtype=np.float64)
        ax.hist(v, bins=_edges, weights=w, color=color, edgecolor="white", linewidth=0.7)
    ax.set_xlim(0.0, _xmax)
    ax.set_title(f"{state_name}\n(n = {n_b:,} buildings)", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold", fontfamily=_title)
    ax.set_ylabel("Percent of buildings (%)", fontsize=11, fontfamily=_body)
    ax.tick_params(axis="both", labelsize=10)
    for tl in ax.get_xticklabels() + ax.get_yticklabels():
        tl.set_fontfamily(_body)
    ax.grid(axis="y", alpha=0.25, linewidth=0.6)
    ax.set_axisbelow(True)


def _sync_hist_axes_ylim_income(ax0, ax1) -> None:
    ymax = 0.0
    for ax in (ax0, ax1):
        for p in ax.patches:
            h = p.get_height()
            if h is not None and np.isfinite(h):
                ymax = max(ymax, float(h))
    ytop = ymax * 1.05 if ymax > 0 else 1.0
    ytop = max(ytop, 0.5)
    ax0.set_ylim(0.0, ytop)
    ax1.set_ylim(0.0, ytop)


_sync_hist_axes_ylim_income(axes[0], axes[1])

axes[1].set_ylabel("")
axes[0].set_xlabel(
    f"Representative annual income (ResStock {RESSTOCK_INCOME_DOLLAR_YEAR}$, run 1+2 annual)",
    fontsize=11,
    fontfamily=_body,
)
axes[1].set_xlabel(
    f"Representative annual income (ResStock {RESSTOCK_INCOME_DOLLAR_YEAR}$, run 1+2 annual)",
    fontsize=11,
    fontfamily=_body,
)
fig.suptitle(
    "Representative annual income — LMI + gas-heated cohort",
    fontsize=13,
    fontweight="bold",
    fontfamily=_title,
    y=1.02,
)
fig.subplots_adjust(left=0.08, right=0.98, top=0.82, bottom=0.18, wspace=0.18)
display_svg(fig)
```


## Total annual energy bill (RI vs. NY)

Same **LMI + gas-heated** cohort and **run 1+2** annual master bills as the bill-mix table above. **Total bill** is the sum of undiscounted **`LMI_BILL_COLS`** (electricity, gas, oil, propane); only buildings with **positive** four-fuel sum are included (same filter as the bill-mix table). The table summarizes the distribution of that total in dollars; the figure is two histograms (RI and NY) with **shared x-axis** limits from the **pooled 99.5th percentile** (rounded to the nearest $100); bar heights are **percent of buildings** in that state (each panel sums to 100%).

```{python}
#| label: tbl-ri-ny-total-bill-summary
#| tbl-cap: "LMI + gas-heated cohort. Distribution summary of total annual undiscounted energy bill (sum of LMI_BILL_COLS, $/yr). RI vs. NY; run 1+2 annual."

from great_tables import GT, md


def _lmi_total_bill_annual_from_dir(path: str) -> pl.DataFrame:
    return (
        pl.scan_parquet(path, hive_partitioning=True)
        .filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
        .select(BLDG_ID, *LMI_BILL_COLS)
        .with_columns(
            pl.sum_horizontal([pl.col(c).fill_null(0) for c in LMI_BILL_COLS]).alias("_bill_sum_four"),
        )
        .filter(pl.col("_bill_sum_four") > 0)
        .collect()
    )


_tot_ann_bill_ri = _lmi_total_bill_annual_from_dir(PATH_MASTER_BILLS_12).filter(
    pl.col(BLDG_ID).is_in(cohort_bldg_ids),
)
_tot_ann_bill_ny = _lmi_total_bill_annual_from_dir(PATH_MASTER_BILLS_12_NY).filter(
    pl.col(BLDG_ID).is_in(cohort_bldg_ids_ny),
)

_ri_tot = _tot_ann_bill_ri["_bill_sum_four"]
_ny_tot = _tot_ann_bill_ny["_bill_sum_four"]

_tbl_total_bill_summary = pl.DataFrame({
    "Statistic": ["Mean", "Median", "Q25", "Q75"],
    "Rhode_Island": [
        float(_ri_tot.mean()),
        float(_ri_tot.median()),
        float(_ri_tot.quantile(0.25)),
        float(_ri_tot.quantile(0.75)),
    ],
    "New_York": [
        float(_ny_tot.mean()),
        float(_ny_tot.median()),
        float(_ny_tot.quantile(0.25)),
        float(_ny_tot.quantile(0.75)),
    ],
})

_gt_total_bill_summary = (
    GT(_tbl_total_bill_summary)
    .tab_header(
        title="Total annual energy bill ($/yr)",
        subtitle=md(
            f"LMI + gas-heated cohort. RI *N* = {_tot_ann_bill_ri.height:,} &nbsp;·&nbsp; "
            f"NY *N* = {_tot_ann_bill_ny.height:,} — sum of electricity + gas + oil + propane bills."
        ),
    )
    .fmt_currency(columns=["Rhode_Island", "New_York"], decimals=0)
    .cols_label(
        Statistic="",
        Rhode_Island="Rhode Island",
        New_York="New York",
    )
    .cols_align(align="right", columns=["Rhode_Island", "New_York"])
)

_gt_total_bill_summary
```

```{python}
#| label: fig-total-bill-distribution-ri-ny
#| fig-cap: "LMI + gas-heated cohort: distribution of total annual undiscounted energy bill (sum of LMI_BILL_COLS), RI vs. NY. Shared x-axis (pooled 99.5th percentile, rounded to $100); y-axis is percent of buildings in that state."

import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np


def _lmi_total_bill_annual_from_dir(path: str) -> pl.DataFrame:
    return (
        pl.scan_parquet(path, hive_partitioning=True)
        .filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
        .select(BLDG_ID, *LMI_BILL_COLS)
        .with_columns(
            pl.sum_horizontal([pl.col(c).fill_null(0) for c in LMI_BILL_COLS]).alias("_bill_sum_four"),
        )
        .filter(pl.col("_bill_sum_four") > 0)
        .collect()
    )


_tot_ann_bill_ri_fig = _lmi_total_bill_annual_from_dir(PATH_MASTER_BILLS_12).filter(
    pl.col(BLDG_ID).is_in(cohort_bldg_ids),
)
_tot_ann_bill_ny_fig = _lmi_total_bill_annual_from_dir(PATH_MASTER_BILLS_12_NY).filter(
    pl.col(BLDG_ID).is_in(cohort_bldg_ids_ny),
)

_ri_b = np.asarray(_tot_ann_bill_ri_fig["_bill_sum_four"].to_numpy(), dtype=np.float64)
_ny_b = np.asarray(_tot_ann_bill_ny_fig["_bill_sum_four"].to_numpy(), dtype=np.float64)
_pool_b = np.concatenate([_ri_b[np.isfinite(_ri_b) & (_ri_b >= 0)], _ny_b[np.isfinite(_ny_b) & (_ny_b >= 0)]])
_hi_b = float(np.percentile(_pool_b, 99.5)) if _pool_b.size else 10_000.0
_hi_b = max(_hi_b, 500.0)
_xmax_b = float(max(np.ceil(_hi_b / 100.0) * 100.0, 1_000.0))
_n_bins_b = min(45, max(20, int(_xmax_b / 250.0)))
_edges_b = np.linspace(0.0, _xmax_b, _n_bins_b + 1)

fig_b2, axes_b2 = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(11, 4.2), sharex=True, sharey=False)
_body_b = "IBM Plex Sans"
_title_b = "GT Planar"
for ax, vals, state_name, color, n_b in zip(
    axes_b2,
    (_ri_b, _ny_b),
    ("Rhode Island", "New York"),
    (SB_COLORS["sky"], SB_COLORS["carrot"]),
    (_ri_b.size, _ny_b.size),
):
    v = np.clip(vals, 0.0, _xmax_b)
    n = int(v.size)
    if n > 0:
        w = np.full(n, 100.0 / n, dtype=np.float64)
        ax.hist(v, bins=_edges_b, weights=w, color=color, edgecolor="white", linewidth=0.7)
    ax.set_xlim(0.0, _xmax_b)
    ax.set_title(f"{state_name}\n(n = {n_b:,} buildings)", fontsize=12, fontweight="bold", fontfamily=_title_b)
    ax.set_ylabel("Percent of buildings (%)", fontsize=11, fontfamily=_body_b)
    ax.tick_params(axis="both", labelsize=10)
    for tl in ax.get_xticklabels() + ax.get_yticklabels():
        tl.set_fontfamily(_body_b)
    ax.grid(axis="y", alpha=0.25, linewidth=0.6)
    ax.set_axisbelow(True)


def _sync_hist_axes_ylim_bill(ax0, ax1) -> None:
    ymax = 0.0
    for ax in (ax0, ax1):
        for p in ax.patches:
            h = p.get_height()
            if h is not None and np.isfinite(h):
                ymax = max(ymax, float(h))
    ytop = ymax * 1.05 if ymax > 0 else 1.0
    ytop = max(ytop, 0.5)
    ax0.set_ylim(0.0, ytop)
    ax1.set_ylim(0.0, ytop)


_sync_hist_axes_ylim_bill(axes_b2[0], axes_b2[1])

axes_b2[1].set_ylabel("")
axes_b2[0].set_xlabel(
    "Total annual bill — elec + gas + oil + propane ($/yr, undiscounted run 1+2)",
    fontsize=11,
    fontfamily=_body_b,
)
axes_b2[1].set_xlabel(
    "Total annual bill — elec + gas + oil + propane ($/yr, undiscounted run 1+2)",
    fontsize=11,
    fontfamily=_body_b,
)
fig_b2.suptitle(
    "Total annual energy bill — LMI + gas-heated cohort",
    fontsize=13,
    fontweight="bold",
    fontfamily=_title_b,
    y=1.02,
)
fig_b2.subplots_adjust(left=0.08, right=0.98, top=0.82, bottom=0.18, wspace=0.18)
display_svg(fig_b2)
```

### Summary tables: fuel shares (RI vs. NY)

**Bill shares** are **percent of the four-fuel annual bill** (`100 ×` that fuel’s `LMI_BILL_COLS` column `÷` sum of the four), for cohort buildings with **positive** bill sum (run 1+2 annual). **Site shares** are **percent of four-fuel site kWh** (same construction as the regression section), for cohort buildings with **positive** summed site kWh. Units are **percentage points** (0–100 scale).

```{python}
#| label: tbl-ri-ny-bill-pct-summary
#| tbl-cap: "LMI + gas-heated cohort. Distribution summary of each fuel’s share of the four-fuel annual bill (%). RI vs. NY; run 1+2, annual, undiscounted bills."

from great_tables import GT, md


def _lmi_bill_share_annual_from_dir(path: str) -> pl.DataFrame:
    return (
        pl.scan_parquet(path, hive_partitioning=True)
        .filter(pl.col("month") == ANNUAL_MONTH)
        .select(BLDG_ID, *LMI_BILL_COLS)
        .with_columns(
            pl.sum_horizontal([pl.col(c).fill_null(0) for c in LMI_BILL_COLS]).alias("_bill_sum_four"),
        )
        .filter(pl.col("_bill_sum_four") > 0)
        .with_columns(
            [
                (100 * pl.col(LMI_BILL_COLS[i]).fill_null(0) / pl.col("_bill_sum_four")).alias(
                    _LMI_BILL_PCT_NAMES[i]
                )
                for i in range(len(LMI_BILL_COLS))
            ]
        )
        .collect()
    )


_lmi_bill_share_annual = _lmi_bill_share_annual_from_dir(PATH_MASTER_BILLS_12).filter(
    pl.col(BLDG_ID).is_in(cohort_bldg_ids),
)
_lmi_bill_share_annual_ny = _lmi_bill_share_annual_from_dir(PATH_MASTER_BILLS_12_NY).filter(
    pl.col(BLDG_ID).is_in(cohort_bldg_ids_ny),
)


def _fuel_share_summary_rows(
    df_ri: pl.DataFrame,
    df_ny: pl.DataFrame,
    cols: tuple[str, ...],
    labels: tuple[str, ...],
) -> pl.DataFrame:
    rows: list[dict[str, float | str]] = []
    for c, lab in zip(cols, labels, strict=True):
        rows.append({
            "Fuel": lab,
            "RI_mean_pct": float(df_ri[c].mean()),
            "RI_median_pct": float(df_ri[c].median()),
            "RI_q25_pct": float(df_ri[c].quantile(0.25)),
            "RI_q75_pct": float(df_ri[c].quantile(0.75)),
            "NY_mean_pct": float(df_ny[c].mean()),
            "NY_median_pct": float(df_ny[c].median()),
            "NY_q25_pct": float(df_ny[c].quantile(0.25)),
            "NY_q75_pct": float(df_ny[c].quantile(0.75)),
        })
    return pl.DataFrame(rows)


_tbl_bill_pct_ri_ny = _fuel_share_summary_rows(
    _lmi_bill_share_annual,
    _lmi_bill_share_annual_ny,
    _LMI_BILL_PCT_NAMES,
    _LMI_BILL_LABELS,
)

_gt_bill_pct_ri_ny = (
    GT(_tbl_bill_pct_ri_ny)
    .tab_header(
        title="Bill mix: each fuel as % of four-fuel annual total",
        subtitle=md(
            f"LMI + gas-heated cohort. RI *N* = {_lmi_bill_share_annual.height:,} &nbsp;·&nbsp; NY *N* = {_lmi_bill_share_annual_ny.height:,} "
            "(sum of `LMI_BILL_COLS` &gt; 0)."
        ),
    )
    .fmt_number(
        columns=[
            "RI_mean_pct",
            "RI_median_pct",
            "RI_q25_pct",
            "RI_q75_pct",
            "NY_mean_pct",
            "NY_median_pct",
            "NY_q25_pct",
            "NY_q75_pct",
        ],
        decimals=1,
    )
    .cols_label(
        RI_mean_pct=md("RI<br>mean"),
        RI_median_pct=md("RI<br>median"),
        RI_q25_pct=md("RI<br>Q25"),
        RI_q75_pct=md("RI<br>Q75"),
        NY_mean_pct=md("NY<br>mean"),
        NY_median_pct=md("NY<br>median"),
        NY_q25_pct=md("NY<br>Q25"),
        NY_q75_pct=md("NY<br>Q75"),
    )
    .cols_align(
        align="right",
        columns=[
            "RI_mean_pct",
            "RI_median_pct",
            "RI_q25_pct",
            "RI_q75_pct",
            "NY_mean_pct",
            "NY_median_pct",
            "NY_q25_pct",
            "NY_q75_pct",
        ],
    )
    .tab_source_note(source_note="All values are percent of annual bill (electricity + gas + oil + propane), $/yr.")
)

_gt_bill_pct_ri_ny
```

```{python}
#| label: tbl-ri-ny-site-pct-summary
#| tbl-cap: "LMI + gas-heated cohort. Distribution summary of each fuel’s share of four-fuel site energy (%). RI vs. NY; ResStock upgrade 0 annual load curves."


def _site_energy_fuel_shares_by_bldg(lf: pl.LazyFrame) -> pl.DataFrame:
    e = LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_NET_ELECTRICITY_CONSUMPTION_COL
    g = LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_NAT_GAS_TOTAL
    p = LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_PROPANE_TOTAL
    fo = LOAD_CURVE_ANNUAL_FUEL_OIL_TOTAL
    return (
        lf.select(BLDG_ID, e, g, p, fo)
        .with_columns(
            pl.col(e).fill_null(0).alias("_e"),
            pl.col(g).fill_null(0).alias("_g"),
            pl.col(p).fill_null(0).alias("_p"),
            pl.col(fo).fill_null(0).alias("_fo"),
        )
        .with_columns((pl.col("_e") + pl.col("_g") + pl.col("_p") + pl.col("_fo")).alias("_tot_site_kwh"))
        .filter(pl.col("_tot_site_kwh") > 0)
        .with_columns(
            (100.0 * pl.col("_e") / pl.col("_tot_site_kwh")).alias("pct_elec_site_energy"),
            (100.0 * pl.col("_g") / pl.col("_tot_site_kwh")).alias("pct_gas_site_energy"),
            (100.0 * pl.col("_p") / pl.col("_tot_site_kwh")).alias("pct_propane_site_energy"),
            (100.0 * pl.col("_fo") / pl.col("_tot_site_kwh")).alias("pct_fuel_oil_site_energy"),
            pl.col("_e").alias("kwh_net_electricity"),
            pl.col("_g").alias("kwh_natural_gas_total"),
            pl.col("_p").alias("kwh_propane_total"),
            pl.col("_fo").alias("kwh_fuel_oil_total"),
        )
        .select(
            BLDG_ID,
            "pct_elec_site_energy",
            "pct_gas_site_energy",
            "pct_propane_site_energy",
            "pct_fuel_oil_site_energy",
            "kwh_net_electricity",
            "kwh_natural_gas_total",
            "kwh_propane_total",
            "kwh_fuel_oil_total",
        )
        .collect()
    )


_ri_site_fuel_shares = _site_energy_fuel_shares_by_bldg(load_curve_annual_ri).filter(
    pl.col(BLDG_ID).is_in(cohort_bldg_ids),
)
_ny_site_fuel_shares = _site_energy_fuel_shares_by_bldg(load_curve_annual_ny).filter(
    pl.col(BLDG_ID).is_in(cohort_bldg_ids_ny),
)

_SITE_PCT_COLS = (
    "pct_elec_site_energy",
    "pct_gas_site_energy",
    "pct_propane_site_energy",
    "pct_fuel_oil_site_energy",
)
_SITE_PCT_LABELS = ("Electricity", "Natural gas", "Propane", "Fuel oil")

_tbl_site_pct_ri_ny = _fuel_share_summary_rows(
    _ri_site_fuel_shares,
    _ny_site_fuel_shares,
    _SITE_PCT_COLS,
    _SITE_PCT_LABELS,
)

_gt_site_pct_ri_ny = (
    GT(_tbl_site_pct_ri_ny)
    .tab_header(
        title="Site energy mix: each fuel as % of four-fuel site kWh",
        subtitle=md(
            f"LMI + gas-heated cohort. RI *N* = {_ri_site_fuel_shares.height:,} &nbsp;·&nbsp; NY *N* = {_ny_site_fuel_shares.height:,} "
            "(positive sum of net elec + gas + propane + fuel oil site kWh)."
        ),
    )
    .fmt_number(
        columns=[
            "RI_mean_pct",
            "RI_median_pct",
            "RI_q25_pct",
            "RI_q75_pct",
            "NY_mean_pct",
            "NY_median_pct",
            "NY_q25_pct",
            "NY_q75_pct",
        ],
        decimals=1,
    )
    .cols_label(
        RI_mean_pct=md("RI<br>mean"),
        RI_median_pct=md("RI<br>median"),
        RI_q25_pct=md("RI<br>Q25"),
        RI_q75_pct=md("RI<br>Q75"),
        NY_mean_pct=md("NY<br>mean"),
        NY_median_pct=md("NY<br>median"),
        NY_q25_pct=md("NY<br>Q25"),
        NY_q75_pct=md("NY<br>Q75"),
    )
    .cols_align(
        align="right",
        columns=[
            "RI_mean_pct",
            "RI_median_pct",
            "RI_q25_pct",
            "RI_q75_pct",
            "NY_mean_pct",
            "NY_median_pct",
            "NY_q25_pct",
            "NY_q75_pct",
        ],
    )
    .tab_source_note(source_note="All values are percent of annual site kWh (net electricity + gas total + propane total + fuel oil total).")
)

_gt_site_pct_ri_ny
```

## Conclusion

The **total annual energy bill** distributions for this cohort line up fairly closely between Rhode Island and New York (see the summary table and histogram above), and the **bill mix** and **site energy mix** tables do not show a large, systematic shift in how fuels show up in bills versus site kWh. By contrast, **representative annual income** in the same LMI + gas-heated cohort is **higher in New York** than in Rhode Island (mean and median both sit above Rhode Island in the income figure and headline diagnostics earlier in the notebook).

Energy burden is **annual energy spending divided by income**. When bills are similar but incomes are lower, **more buildings cross a fixed burden threshold** (here, 6%) even if per-building energy costs are not dramatically different. That pattern is a strong reason Rhode Island would show a **higher share of energy-burdened `bldg_id`s** than New York in these tables, without needing a large gap in modeled bills.

Both cohorts are defined with the same logical filter — **`is_lmi_any` in the master bills, gas heat, positive income** — but that flag is produced using **state-specific LMI rules** in the bill pipeline. So “LMI” does not mean an identical income cut or enrollment rule in RI versus NY; it means each building cleared that state’s LMI classification. **Differences in how New York and Rhode Island LMI buildings are classified**, together with **lower representative incomes among RI LMI buildings in this sample**, plausibly explain much of the gap in burden shares relative to differences in total bills alone.
